author: pietroGru
body: #### PR description: While looking at the HLT/Vertexing plots in the DQM directory (i.e., `HLT/Vertexing/hltPixelVertices/Alignment/dxyVsEta_pt10` https://cern.ch/7khox), we realized that the horizontal labels in the `TProfile`s produced by the `GeneralPurposeVertexAnalyzer` do indicate the default pT>1 GeV cut also for the plotted quantities related to tracks with pT>10 GeV cut. From conversation with @elusian and @mmasciov, we realized that the pT cut is actually applied (i.e., see [GeneralPurposeVertexAnalyzer.cc#L575-L584](https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/blob/CMSSW_20_1_X/Alignment/OfflineValidation/plugins/GeneralPurposeVertexAnalyzer.cc#L575-L584) for the `*_pt10` filled `TH1D`s and `IPMonitoring`s), so this is likely a typo which this PR proposes to fix - by formatting the pT cut in the labels. #### PR validation: The code compiles ok
title: Update `GeneralPurposeVertexAnalyzer` axes labels accordingly to track pT cut
